chapter_title.ar: باب الْمَرْأَةِ تَمُوتُ وَ فِي بَطْنِهَا صَبِيٌّ يَتَحَرَّكُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 72 – The woman dies and in her belly is a moving child

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/129)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/129)
text: الْمَرْأَةِ تَمُوتُ وَ يَتَحَرَّكُ الْوَلَدُ فِي بَطْنِهَا أَ يُشَقُّ بَطْنُهَا وَ يُخْرَجُ الْوَلَدُ قَالَ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ وَ يُخَاطُ بَطْنُهَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from one of his companions,

from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the dying woman and the child is moving in her belly, should her belly be split and the child taken out?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, and her belly would be stitched’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from certain persons of his people who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, was asked, ‘If a woman dies with a child moving in her womb, is her belly to be opened to take the child out?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, and her belly is stitched to close.”
